front: Novel: in this novel, the doctor's wife is the only person immune to an epidemic of 'white sickness' that blinds an entire city
Novel: characters in this novel include 'the girl with the dark glasses,' 'the old man with the black eye patch,' and 'the boy with the squint'
Novel: in this novel, a car thief develops a leg infection after being kicked with a stiletto heel by the girl with dark glasses
Novel: in this novel, the doctor's wife stabs the 'King of Ward Three' in the neck with a pair of scissors after he demands women sleep with his ward's men for food
Novel: in this novel, a 'dog of tears' comforts the protagonist by licking her face in the rain and joins her group; the dog later kills and eats an old woman's hen
Novel: this novel opens with a man who fails to drive at a green stoplight — the first victim of the title condition
Novel: in this novel, blood mixes with milk after soldiers shoot internees escaping from a mental hospital
Novel: in this novel, the protagonist discovers that statues of saints in a church have had their eyes covered with paint or cloth
Author: this author used his dog Camões as inspiration for the 'dog of tears' character in his most famous novel
Novel: in this sequel to Blindness, 83% of voters turn in blank ballots; the government brands them as terrorists

Novel: in this novel, death is personified as a woman with a lowercase-'d' name who sends violet-colored letters one week before people die, then falls in love with a cellist who keeps returning her letters
Novel: in this novel, a non-Italian 'maphia' (spelled with 'ph') smuggles people across a country's borders so they can die, and offers funeral directors protection from 'eternal life activists'
Novel: in this novel, a character nicknamed 'fifty-eight seconds' is so named for his love of Chopin's Butterfly étude
Novel: in this novel, Jesus is apprenticed to a shepherd who is the Devil in disguise and is forced to sacrifice his favorite sheep
back: The Gospel According to Jesus Christ (Saramago)
work: The Gospel According to Jesus Christ

front: Novel: in this novel, Jesus meets an old man and a shepherd — who turn out to be God and Satan — in the middle of a lake
back: The Gospel According to Jesus Christ (Saramago)
work: The Gospel According to Jesus Christ

front: Novel: in this novel, Jesus's father is executed after trying to help Ananias, and the protagonist claims his father's sandals
back: The Gospel According to Jesus Christ (Saramago)
work: The Gospel According to Jesus Christ

front: Novel: in this novel, a Pessoa heteronym returns to Lisbon from Brazil and frequently converses with Pessoa's ghost
back: The Year of the Death of Ricardo Reis (Saramago)
work: The Year of the Death of Ricardo Reis

front: Novel: in this novel, the protagonist has affairs with the chambermaid Lydia and the socialite Marcenda, whose paralyzed arm is treated by her father to cover visits to his mistress
back: The Year of the Death of Ricardo Reis (Saramago)
work: The Year of the Death of Ricardo Reis

front: Novel: this novel ends with the protagonist putting on his jacket and walking to a cemetery to follow Pessoa's ghost

front: Novel: in this novel, the Iberian Peninsula breaks off from Europe and floats across the Atlantic after Joana Carda's actions
Novel: this author's final novel reimagines the Biblical title character as a time traveler who becomes Lilith's lover and stops Abraham from sacrificing Isaac

Novel: in this novel, the time-traveling protagonist witnesses God's cruelty, including the destruction of Sodom and Gomorrah, and is ultimately doomed to live alone after killing everyone on Noah's Ark
Novel: in this novel, the bureaucrat Senhor José works at the 'Central Registry of Births, Marriages, and Deaths' and becomes obsessed with an unknown woman after accidentally grabbing her index card
Novel: in this novel, a Mesopotamia-obsessed history teacher named Tertuliano discovers his doppelgänger while watching a film
Novel: in this novel, the proofreader Raimundo Silva inserts the word 'not' into a medieval history manuscript and falls in love with his supervisor Dr. Maria Sara
back: The History of the Siege of Lisbon (Saramago)
work: The History of the Siege of Lisbon

front: Novel: in this novel, Bartholomew Lorenzo invents the Passarola, a flying machine used by the title lovers to escape the Inquisition
Novel: in this novel, King João III sends an elephant named Solomon to Archduke Maximilian; Solomon's keeper Subhro has his name changed to Fritz in Austria
Author: this author's first novel, about a widow who has an affair with her brother-in-law, was retitled Land of Sin
Author: Margaret Jull Costa is the principal English translator of this Portuguese author, winning awards for translations of All the Names and The Elephant's Journey
